evidence.claim: ASTM F1955-22 'Standard Specification for Backpack Frames' provides recognized US standards for hiking backpack frame strength, load distribution, and harness components.
REI Expert Advice on backpack volume measurement and packing.

criteria.name: volume_liters
criteria.notes: Day hikes 18-30L; overnight 40-50L; multi-day 55-75L. Subtract 10-15% for real usable volume (E1).

confabulationPatterns.reality: 'Ultralight' is loose; for 65L volume, under 2 lbs is ultralight (Hyperlite, ZPacks); 3-4 lbs is 'lightweight'; over 4 lbs is conventional. Always check base weight in oz.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the ultralight claim supported by a sub-32 oz weight spec?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Most hipbelts fail to transfer loads effectively above 35-40 lbs regardless of claimed rating. Load-transfer quality matters more than rating.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the heavy-load rating paired with a load-transfer score or just a capacity claim?

counterfeitSignals.signal: Osprey / Gregory pack below 40% of retailer price from marketplace seller

compatibilityQuestions: Torso length — fit matters more than size. Measure spine C7 to iliac crest.
Are you carrying bear cannister / climbing rope / photography gear? Volume adders.

repairability.notes: Osprey has a lifetime 'All-Mighty Guarantee'. Gregory, Deuter also strong. Budget brands often replace-don't-repair.
